text: Greedy Pattern: Activity selection / interval scheduling. Select maximum number of non-overlapping intervals. Sort by end time. Greedily pick interval with earliest end time; skip any that conflict. Template: sort by end; prev_end=-inf; for s,e in intervals: if s>=prev_end: count+=1; prev_end=e. Time: O(n log n). Works because earliest-ending choice leaves maximum room for future intervals.

Greedy Pattern: Interval merging. Merge all overlapping intervals. Sort by start time. For each interval, if it overlaps with last merged (start <= last_end), extend last_end. Else add new interval. Time: O(n log n). Use for: merge intervals, insert interval, meeting rooms I. Key: sorted order ensures we only check adjacent intervals.

Greedy Pattern: Meeting rooms II (minimum rooms). Find minimum conference rooms needed. Use two pointers on sorted start and end arrays. If next meeting starts before earliest-ending meeting ends, need new room. Else, reuse room. Alternative: min-heap of end times. Time: O(n log n). Generalizes to: minimum number of platforms, CPU scheduling.

Greedy Pattern: Jump game (can reach end). Track max_reach. For each index i: if i > max_reach, return False (can't reach here). Update max_reach = max(max_reach, i+nums[i]). Return True at end. Time: O(n), Space: O(1). For minimum jumps: track current_reach and next_reach, increment jumps when current_reach is exhausted.

Greedy Pattern: Gas station circular tour. Find starting station for complete circular tour. If total gas < total cost, impossible. Otherwise, a valid start always exists. Greedy: if tank goes negative at station i, reset tank and set start=i+1. Proof: the segment from current start to i is invalid; start must be after i. Time: O(n).

Greedy Pattern: Assign cookies to children. Maximize number of satisfied children (greed factor <= cookie size). Sort both arrays. Two pointers: if smallest available cookie >= least greedy unsatisfied child, assign it. Move both pointers. Else try next larger cookie. Greedy works: smallest cookie that satisfies is optimal (saves larger cookies for greedier children). Time: O(n log n).

Greedy Pattern: Task scheduler with cooldown. Schedule tasks with n cooldown between same task. Count frequencies. Sort descending. Max frequency task dictates idle slots: (max_freq-1)*n idle slots needed. Reduce by other task counts (min(count, max_freq-1) fill idle slots). Total time = max(total_tasks, idle + total_tasks). Time: O(tasks).

Greedy Pattern: Minimum number of arrows to burst balloons. Each balloon is interval [start, end]. Find minimum arrows to burst all. Sort by end. Shoot at end of first balloon; removes all overlapping. Move to next non-burst balloon. Same as interval scheduling. Time: O(n log n). Key insight: shooting at rightmost point of current balloon maximizes coverage.

Greedy Pattern: Lemonade change. Give change greedily. For $5: bank $5. For $10: give $5 change, bank $10. For $20: prefer $10+$5 over $5+$5+$5 (saves $5 bills which are more versatile). Return False if can't make change. Greedy: always use largest denomination first when making change. Time: O(n).

Greedy Pattern: Partition labels. Each letter must appear in only one partition. For each character, track last occurrence. Extend current partition to include last occurrence of all characters seen so far. When index reaches partition end, finalize. Template: last={c:i for i,c in enumerate(s)}; start=end=0; for i,c in enumerate(s): end=max(end,last[c]); if i==end: record partition. Time: O(n).

Greedy Pattern: Candy problem (two-pass). Each child with higher rating than neighbor gets more candy. Initialize all with 1. Left pass: if ratings[i]>ratings[i-1]: candies[i]=candies[i-1]+1. Right pass: if ratings[i]>ratings[i+1]: candies[i]=max(candies[i], candies[i+1]+1). Sum all. Time: O(n). Greedy ensures minimum total while satisfying constraints.

Greedy Pattern: Fractional knapsack. Items have weight and value; fractional items allowed. Sort by value/weight ratio descending. Greedily take items with highest ratio. If item fits fully, take it; else take fraction. Time: O(n log n). Unlike 0/1 knapsack (DP), greedy works here because fractional items are allowed, enabling optimal exchange argument.

Greedy Pattern: Huffman coding. Build optimal prefix-free binary code. Use min-heap of frequencies. Repeatedly merge two lowest-frequency nodes into parent with combined frequency. Build tree bottom-up. Characters closer to root get shorter codes. Time: O(n log n). Greedy works because combining two smallest always minimizes expected code length.

Greedy Pattern: Minimum cost to connect ropes. Connect all ropes with minimum total cost; cost = sum of two rope lengths being connected. Use min-heap. Always merge two shortest ropes first. Total cost accumulated. Time: O(n log n). Same as Huffman coding structure. Greedy works by exchange argument: merging smallest ropes minimizes repeated additions.

Greedy Pattern: Stock trading (multiple transactions). Buy and sell stock for maximum profit, unlimited transactions. Greedy: collect every upward slope. If prices[i] > prices[i-1], add the difference. Equivalently, buy on every dip and sell on every peak. Total profit = sum of all positive differences. Time: O(n), Space: O(1).

Greedy Pattern: Reorganize string (no adjacent same chars). If any character appears more than (n+1)//2 times, impossible. Otherwise, greedily place most frequent character, alternating. Use max-heap: place highest frequency char at current position, then next highest. Or: fill even indices first, then odd indices with characters sorted by frequency. Time: O(n log k) with heap.

Greedy Pattern: Minimum number of platforms (train schedule). Find peak concurrent trains. Events: +1 for arrival, -1 for departure. Sort events by time. Scan events, track current platforms in use, record maximum. Or: sort arrivals and departures separately, use two-pointer merge. Time: O(n log n). Generalizes: minimum resources for overlapping intervals.

Greedy Pattern: Maximize sum after K negations. Sort array. Greedily flip negatives from most negative to least negative. If k remaining after all negatives flipped, flip smallest absolute value k%2 times. Time: O(n log n). Greedy works: flipping the most negative number gives the biggest gain; odd remaining flips cancel on smallest element.

Greedy Pattern: Non-overlapping intervals (minimum removals). Minimize intervals to remove so no two overlap. Greedy: keep maximum non-overlapping intervals (activity selection). Answer = total - maximum kept. Sort by end time. Greedily keep intervals that end earliest. Any interval conflicting with kept ones must be removed. Time: O(n log n).

Greedy Pattern: Greedy proof technique (exchange argument). To prove greedy is optimal: assume optimal solution differs from greedy at some step. Show that swapping the optimal choice with the greedy choice does not worsen the solution. Repeat until optimal matches greedy. Common in: scheduling (sort by deadline), coins, interval problems. Always verify greedy with exchange argument or counterexample.
